anchor.snippet: a result has statistical significance when a result at least as

annotations.note: Grep for 'significance' and 'hypothesis test' in Mathlib returned no relevant hits; statistical inference is not formalized.

annotations.label: Significance level

anchor.snippet: is the probability of the study rejecting the null hypothesis, given that the null hypothesis is true

annotations.note: No significance level / hypothesis testing infrastructure found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: p-value

anchor.snippet: is the probability of obtaining a result at least as extreme, given that the null hypothesis is true

annotations.note: Grep for pValue / p_value returned no statistical hits; p-values are not formalized in Mathlib.

anchor.snippet: The result is said to be statistically significant , by the standards of the study, when

annotations.note: The p ≤ α decision rule requires unformalized hypothesis-testing machinery.

annotations.label: Significance level as false positive probability
annotations.note: No formalization of α as type-I-error threshold in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Null hypothesis

anchor.snippet: The null hypothesis is the hypothesis that no effect exists in the phenomenon being studied

annotations.note: Grep for 'null hypothesis' in Mathlib returned no files.

annotations.label: p-value (hypothesis testing)

anchor.snippet: which is the probability of observing an effect of the same magnitude or more extreme given that the null hypothesis is true

annotations.note: Same result as the lead p-value entry; p-values are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Effect size

anchor.snippet: Effect size is a measure of a study's practical significance
